name: OWASP Top 10 Security Scan
description: Performs a source-level security review against the OWASP Top 10, classifies findings by severity and CWE, and produces a prioritized remediation report with concrete code references.
author: ClaudeStudio Code Quality Pack

steps.title: Map attack surface
steps.prompt: Detect the stack ({{language}}, 'auto' = infer from files) in {{target_dir}} and map the attack surface: HTTP routes/controllers, input boundaries (request bodies, query params, headers, file uploads), database access, authentication/session handling, secrets/config, deserialization, template rendering, and outbound calls. List the relevant files and entry points.

steps.title: Scan against OWASP Top 10
steps.prompt: Review the mapped code for the OWASP Top 10 (2021): A01 Broken Access Control, A02 Cryptographic Failures, A03 Injection (SQL/NoSQL/command/template), A04 Insecure Design, A05 Security Misconfiguration, A06 Vulnerable/Outdated Components, A07 Identification & Authentication Failures, A08 Software & Data Integrity Failures (incl. insecure deserialization), A09 Security Logging & Monitoring Failures, A10 Server-Side Request Forgery. For each finding give: OWASP category, CWE id, file and line, a short proof-of-concept or trigger condition, and a severity (critical/high/medium/low) with CVSS-style reasoning. Suppress findings below {{min_severity}}.

steps.title: Remediation report
steps.prompt: Produce a Markdown security report: 'Executive Summary' with a severity count table, 'Findings' (sorted by severity, each with category, CWE, location, impact, and a concrete remediation including a corrected code snippet), 'Quick Wins', and 'Methodology & Scope'. State clearly that this is a static, best-effort review and not a substitute for a full penetration test.
